# UC-13.6.3 · Grafana Unified Alerting Rule Evaluation Duration and Timeout Rate

## Description

Heavy PromQL or high-cardinality queries in alert rules stall the Grafana scheduler, delaying pages across unrelated services. Tracking evaluation duration and timeout strings isolates toxic rule groups before on-call trust erodes.

## Value

Protects paging reliability and prevents alert silences caused by backlog, not silence configs.

## Implementation

If only logs exist, parse `eval_duration_ms` via `rex`. Export Grafana internal metrics (`grafana_alerting_rule_evaluations_total`) via Prometheus remote_write to Splunk Observability if available. Tune thresholds per org tier.

## SPL

```spl
index=observability sourcetype="grafana:alerting"
| eval slow_eval=if(isnotnull(eval_duration_ms) AND eval_duration_ms > 5000,1,0)
| eval timeout_hit=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)context deadline exceeded|evaluation timeout|cancelled"),1,0)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count as evals, sum(slow_eval) as slow_cnt, sum(timeout_hit) as timeouts, median(eval_duration_ms) as med_ms by _time, rule_group, org_id
| eval slow_rate=round(100*slow_cnt/nullif(evals,0),2)
| where timeouts > 0 OR slow_rate > 10
| sort -timeouts
```

## Visualization

Heatmap (rule_group × eval ms), timechart of timeout count, table of worst med_ms.
